definition: All major economies are aging concurrently for the first time in history, eliminating the demographic arbitrage that previously allowed capital and labor to flow between demographically divergent economies. This is an unprecedented structural condition with no historical template.
evidence: All major economies aging simultaneously — unprecedented in history
Eliminates historical demographic arbitrage between economies at different life-cycle stages
Eastern European depopulation cascades: emigration reduces dynamism, driving further emigration
Markets mispriced growth by 1-2pp annually for at least a decade across 7 demographic transitions
Japan + Europe + China + US = ~50-55% of global GDP with working-age shares declining
No prior aging episode coincided with primary capital destinations also aging
Africa adds ~650M working-age, India ~200M, but institutional capacity today is insufficient at required scale
Institutional development in India/Africa is nonlinear; 2025 snapshot should not be projected as fixed
open_questions: Does simultaneous aging imply globally lower r* with no offset mechanism?
Which countries or regions retain genuine demographic advantages?
How does simultaneous aging affect global capital allocation patterns?
Can India's capital market depth develop fast enough to serve as safety valve?
Will Africa's institutional quality metrics improve on the 20-year horizon?
What financial predictions, if any, can be made from an N=0 configuration?
